contentBlocks.original:
जय जय श्री शनिदेव भक्तन हितकारी ।
सूर्य पुत्र प्रभु छाया महतारी ॥
जय जय श्री शनि देव ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jaya jaya śrī śanidēva bhaktana hitakārī ।
sūrya putra prabhu Chāyā mahatārī ॥
jaya jaya śrī śani dēva ।
contentBlocks.translation: Victory, victory to Śrī Śanideva — O benefactor of devotees! O son of the Sun — whose great mother is Chāyā. Victory, victory to Śrī Śani Deva.
contentBlocks.meanings: jaya jaya: victory, victory | śrī śani-deva: Śrī Śani (Saturn deity) | bhaktana hitakārī: benefactor of devotees | sūrya putra: son of the Sun | prabhu: lord | chāyā mahātārī: whose great mother is Chāyā (Shadow, the second wife of the Sun)

contentBlocks.original:
श्याम अङ्ग वक्र-दृष्टि चतुर्भुजा धारी ।
नी लाम्बर धार नाथ गज की असवारी ॥
जय जय श्री शनि देव ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śyāma aṅga vakra-dṛṣṭi chaturbhujā dhārī ।
nī lāmbara dhāra nātha gaja kī asavārī ॥
jaya jaya śrī śani dēva ।
contentBlocks.translation: Dark-bodied, with a crooked glance, bearer of four arms — wearing dark blue robes, O lord — riding an elephant. Victory, victory to Śrī Śani Deva.
contentBlocks.meanings: śyāma aṅga: dark body | vakra-dṛṣṭi: crooked/sideways glance | catur-bhujā dhārī: bearer of four arms | nīlāmbara: dark blue robes | nātha: O lord | gaja kī asavārī: riding an elephant

contentBlocks.original:
क्रीट मुकुट शीश राजित दिपत है लिलारी ।
मुक्तन की माला गले शोभित बलिहारी ॥
जय जय श्री शनि देव ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
krīṭa mukuṭa śīśa rājita dipata hai lilārī ।
muktana kī mālā galē śōbhita balihārī ॥
jaya jaya śrī śani dēva ।
contentBlocks.translation: On the head — a jeweled crown, shining on the forehead — a garland of pearls at the throat, beautiful, incomparable. Victory, victory to Śrī Śani Deva.
contentBlocks.meanings: krīṭa mukuṭa: jeweled crown | śīśa rājit: adorning the head | dipat: shining | lilārī: on the forehead | muktana kī mālā: garland of pearls | gale: at the throat | śobhit: beautiful | balihārī: incomparable/wonderful

contentBlocks.original:
मोदक मिष्ठान पान चढ़त हैं सुपारी ।
लोहा तिल तेल उड़द महिषी अति प्यारी ॥
जय जय श्री शनि देव ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mōdaka miṣṭhāna pāna chaḍha़ta haiṃ supārī ।
lōhā tila tēla uḍa़da mahiṣī ati pyārī ॥
jaya jaya śrī śani dēva ।
contentBlocks.translation: Modaka sweets, betel leaf, and betel nut are offered to you — iron, sesame, oil, black lentil (urad), and black female buffalo are especially dear to you. Victory, victory to Śrī Śani Deva.
contentBlocks.meanings: modaka miṣṭhān: modaka sweets | pān: betel leaf | caḍhat haiṃ: are offered | supārī: betel nut | loha: iron | tila: sesame | tela: oil | uḍad: urad/black lentil | mahiṣī: female buffalo | ati pyārī: especially dear

contentBlocks.original:
देव दनुज ऋषि मुनि सुमिरत नर नारी ।
विश्वनाथ धरत ध्यान शरण हैं तुम्हारी ॥
जय जय श्री शनि देव भक्तन हितकारी ॥
जय जय श्री शनि देव ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dēva danuja ṛṣi muni sumirata nara nārī ।
viśvanātha dharata dhyāna śaraṇa haiṃ tumhārī ॥
jaya jaya śrī śani dēva bhaktana hitakārī ॥
jaya jaya śrī śani dēva ।
contentBlocks.translation: Gods, demons, sages, and saints — men and women — all remember [you]. Viśvanātha meditates on you — [we] are in your refuge. Victory, victory to Śrī Śani Deva.
contentBlocks.meanings: deva: gods | danuja: demons | ṛṣi muni: sages and saints | sumirat: remembering | nara nārī: men and women | viśvanātha: the lord of all (here possibly the poet's pen name or referring to Śiva as Viśvanātha) | dharat dhyāna: meditates | śaraṇa haiṃ tumhārī: we are in your refuge
